name: Soutelo de Montes - Forcarei - return of Rio Lerez
name_original: Soutelo de Montes - Forcarei - vuelta de Rio Lerez
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Forcarei traverse a diverse landscape within the province of Pontevedra, Spain. The region features varied terrain, including green mountains, forests, and quiet paved roads, offering a range of gradients for cyclists. Routes often lead through woodlands and alongside rivers, providing varied scenery. Cyclists can expect roads with varying gradients, suitable for both leisurely rides and more demanding training. Best road cycling routes around Forcarei The most popular road cycling route is Soutelo de Montes - Forcarei - return of Rio Lerez, a 22.6 miles (36.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ponte Taboada – Church of Vila de Cruces loop from Silleda, a moderate 33.4 miles (53.7 km) path. This route offers a mix of natural scenery and historical points of interest, including the Taboada Medieval Bridge. Local road cyclists also love the Railway Viaduct – A Carixa Bridge loop from Silleda, a 30.9 miles (49.7 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Forcarei is defined by varied terrain, diverse forests, and quiet paved roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging climbs. The routes in Forcarei are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Forcarei's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Forcarei?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Forcarei documented on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes have been explored by more than 500 road cyclists.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for road cycling in Forcarei?
A: Road cycling in Forcarei offers a diverse landscape, characterized by green meadows, mountains, and varied terrain. You'll find a mix of quiet paved roads, forested areas, and routes alongside rivers. The gradients vary, providing options for both leisurely rides and more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or easy rides in Forcarei?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many of the moderate routes feature sections with varying gradients that can be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous ride. The region's quiet paved roads are generally suitable for a relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What about more challenging road cycling routes around Forcarei?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, Forcarei offers several difficult road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Tres Caños – Alto do Paraño loop from Cerdedo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 92 km with significant elevation gain, testing endurance and climbing skills.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in the Forcarei area?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Forcarei are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20495517" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Taboada – Church of Vila de Cruces loop from Silleda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40375590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Railway Viaduct – A Carixa Bridge loop from Silleda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Forcarei?
A: The routes often pass by interesting points. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6611782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anonymous Pilgrim Statue&lt;/a&gt; near the Church of Santiago de Taboada, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3854687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taboada Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. Natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6628593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memorial Tree in the Forest&lt;/a&gt; also add to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, as part of a mountainous region, road cycling routes around Forcarei offer opportunities for breathtaking and panoramic views. Keep an eye out for spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2730483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Estuaries Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides impressive vistas of the surrounding countryside.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Forcarei?
A: The road cycling routes in Forcarei are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the quiet paved roads, and the diverse forests that make for an immersive experience with nature.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Forcarei?
A: The region's 'green of the meadows and mountains' suggests that spring and early autumn would be ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be good, especially for rides through forested areas that provide shade.

Q: Are there any hidden gems or less crowded road cycling routes in Forcarei?
A: Forcarei is known for its 'hidden gems waiting to be explored.' Many of the routes lead through quiet country roads and diverse forests, offering a peaceful cycling experience away from heavy traffic. Exploring the less-traveled paths often reveals unique natural beauty and tranquility.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the region's towns and villages, such as Silleda, often have local establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6170367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Baia Church in Silleda&lt;/a&gt; is near a leisure area that could serve as a pleasant stop.

Q: What is the average length and duration of road cycling routes in Forcarei?
A: The road cycling routes in Forcarei vary significantly in length and duration. For instance, a popular route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969174082/soutelo-de-montes-forcarei-return-of-rio-lerez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soutelo de Montes - Forcarei - return of Rio Lerez&lt;/a&gt; is about 36 km and takes nearly 2 hours, while longer routes can extend to over 90 km and require several hours to complete.
tips: It is currently closed most of the time, except for occasional events.

Remains of a watchtower or part of a warning system through the mountains built with slate with a total height of about 10 meters. Its construction possibly dates from the end of the 15th century or the beginning of the 16th.
Stone chapel next to the Lerez river
